_meta.description: Long-form article body: intro paragraph, subheading, body copy, pull quote, and a closing paragraph

blocks.text: Every design system starts with the same problem: inconsistency. Colors drift. Spacing values multiply. Component names diverge across files. The system is the solution, but only if you build it deliberately.

Tokens are the primitive values your system is built on: color, spacing, typography, radius, shadow. When you define those first, every component that references them stays consistent automatically. Change a token and everything composed from it updates.
"Define the primitives first. The components will follow."
Name things before you build them

Naming is architecture. A button named btn-primary tells you what role it plays. A color named color-accent tells you where it belongs. Before writing a single line of CSS or building a single component, write down your naming rules and stick to them.
The best design systems are not the most complete ones. They are the ones people actually use. Start small, stay consistent, and document as you go.
